name: Kingdom of Galicia–Volhynia (c. 1199–1349)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Galician arms
the gold lion of Galicia, in continuous use as a regional emblem since the 13th century

description: A blue field charged with a gold lion rampant — the heraldic arms of the medieval Kingdom of Galicia–Volhynia.
history: The gold lion on an azure field is the heraldic emblem of the medieval Kingdom of Galicia–Volhynia (c. 1199–1349), a Rus' principality covering much of western and southwestern Ukraine. The kingdom's heraldry is the direct source of the modern Ukrainian national flag's blue-and-yellow palette: the Constitution of the Ukrainian People's Republic (1918) explicitly grounded its choice of national colours in this medieval ancestor. The 'flag' shown here is a reconstructed armorial banner, since medieval Rus' principalities used hetmans' personal banners rather than territorial flags in the modern sense.

features.symbolism: the heraldic ancestor of Ukraine's modern blue-and-yellow flag
